subject: LU-4648 ldiskfs: delete external EA in another transaction

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(6 comments)

Sorry, after thinking about this more, I think the approach of making a separate list of inodes is not going to work properly.  Adding the main inode to the orphan list and then deleting each xattr inode one-at-a-time within the main transaction, restarting the transaction if needed, should work.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch has passed auto-tests, but what could be the best way to specifically test its new code ? What can use big xattr space on OSS ? At least it will be used on MDS with heavy striping ?

looks like the patch needs some updates

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

if I understand correctly, the change has been done to ldiskfs_xattr_delete_inode(), but I find this wrong because ldiskfs_orphan_del() is called before that. so it sounds possible that we first remove the main inode from the orphan list, then we go to drop large EAs and given we restart transaction, we can get the main inode leaked - it's not destroyed yet and not on the orphan list already?

Patch Set 3:

> but I find this wrong because ldiskfs_orphan_del() is called before that.

The patch has put ldiskfs_orphan_del() after ldiskfs_free_inode()->ldiskfs_xattr_delete_inode()

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

right, missed that important change.. but please check another scenario described inline.

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

hard to judge at the moment, but probably it's easier to release large EAs before we start to free the main inode - we don't have a transaction at that point, the main inode is still on the orphan list and we can work out large EAs one by one.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

this patchset tries to delete inode's xattr first in another transaction other than of the main inode.

Patch Set 9: Code-Review-1

there is a issue in this patch which causes sanity 17m test fail. The problem is that I vfs_dq_drop() of the main inode too early so that it can not record the block freed by the following data truncate.

I'll revise and update a new patchset.

I'll add it back in to double check to make sure it really was the culprit

parents.subject: LU-6085 mdt: return valid attribute only to client

commit.subject: LU-4648 ldiskfs: delete external EA in another transaction
commit.message:
LU-4648 ldiskfs: delete external EA in another transaction

The transaction credit of ldiskfs_delete_inode() does not count in
the external EA inodes deletion, which could lead to transaction
credit deficiency.

This patch collect the inode's xattr inodes and put them to orphan
list, and later delete them in another transaction after deleting the
main inode.
